# Test MCP tools integration

# Create test file
echo 'mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"init","id":1} # 1000.000' > test1.mcp
echo 'mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{}} # 1001.000' >> test1.mcp
echo 'mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ping","id":2} # 1002.000' >> test1.mcp
echo 'mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":{"message":"pong"}} # 1003.000' >> test1.mcp

# Test mcpcat
exec mcpcat test1.mcp
stdout 'mcp-recv'
stdout 'mcp-send'

# Test mcp-sort (strip timestamps)
exec mcp-sort -strip test1.mcp
stdout '\[TIMESTAMP\]'

# Create a second file for diff
cp test1.mcp test2.mcp
echo 'mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"new","id":3} # 1004.000' >> test2.mcp

# Test mcpdiff (exit code should be 1 for differences)
! exec mcpdiff test1.mcp test2.mcp
stdout 'new'

# Test mcpspy with a simple echo command
echo 'test' > input.txt
exec mcpspy -f spy.mcp cat input.txt
stdout 'test'
exists spy.mcp

# Test mcp-shadow behavior with a simple trace
echo '# mcptrace:v1 compare=true' > shadow.mcp
echo 'mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{},"id":1} # 1000.000 spanid=aaa1' >> shadow.mcp
echo 'mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{}}} # 1001.000 spanid=aaa2' >> shadow.mcp
echo 'mcp-send-shadow {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{"tools":true}}} # 1001.100 spanid=bbb2 linksto=aaa2' >> shadow.mcp

# Test mcpdiff in compare mode
exec mcpdiff -compare shadow.mcp
stdout 'tools'

# Test colorization with mcpcat on shadow trace
exec mcpcat -color=never shadow.mcp
stdout 'mcp-send-shadow'

# Chain mcp-sort with mcpcat  
exec mcp-sort -strip test1.mcp
stdout '\[TIMESTAMP\]'

# Verify all created files
exists test1.mcp
exists test2.mcp
exists spy.mcp
exists shadow.mcp
